commands.name: apt
commands.description: High-level package manager for Debian-based systems

patterns.text: update the list of available packages
patterns.explanation: Refreshes the package index from configured repositories
Installs the newest versions of all currently installed packages
Downloads and installs nginx and its dependencies

patterns.text: remove a package but keep its config files
patterns.explanation: Uninstalls the package but leaves configuration files

patterns.text: search for a package by name or description
patterns.explanation: Searches the package database for matching packages

patterns.text: install a package without prompts in a script
patterns.explanation: Non-interactive install suitable for automation and Dockerfiles

patterns.text: completely remove a package and its config
patterns.explanation: Removes the package along with all configuration files
Removes cached .deb files to free disk space
Uninstalls packages that were installed as dependencies but are no longer needed
Upgrades packages and handles dependency changes intelligently
Installs a package directly from a .deb file
Shows every installed package with version and description

patterns.text: find which package owns a file
patterns.explanation: Identifies which package installed the given file

patterns.text: list files installed by a package
patterns.explanation: Shows every file installed by the nginx package

commands.name: yum
commands.description: Package manager for RHEL/CentOS 7 and older

patterns.text: install a package on CentOS or RHEL
patterns.explanation: Installs Apache HTTP server and its dependencies
Upgrades all packages to the latest available versions
Searches package names and descriptions for matches
Uninstalls the specified package
Shows all currently installed packages

commands.name: dnf
commands.description: Next-generation package manager for Fedora and RHEL 8+

patterns.explanation: Installs the GCC compiler and its dependencies
Searches repositories for packages matching the query
Displays details like version, size, and description

patterns.text: remove a package and its unused dependencies
patterns.explanation: Removes the package and any orphaned dependencies
Shows all currently enabled package repositories

patterns.text: install a package on Arch Linux
patterns.explanation: Syncs and installs the package from the repositories
Synchronizes databases and upgrades all packages
Searches the sync databases for matching packages

patterns.text: remove a package and its unused dependencies
patterns.explanation: Removes the package, its config, and orphaned dependencies
Shows packages that were explicitly installed by the user

commands.name: brew
commands.description: Package manager for macOS and Linux (Homebrew)

patterns.text: install a command-line tool on macOS
patterns.explanation: Downloads and installs the formula
Installs a desktop application as a cask

patterns.text: update Homebrew and all installed packages
patterns.explanation: Fetches the latest formulae and upgrades all packages

patterns.text: search for a package in Homebrew
patterns.explanation: Searches for formulae and casks matching the query

patterns.text: uninstall a package and clean up
patterns.explanation: Removes the formula and deletes old cached downloads

commands.name: snap
commands.description: Package manager for snap packages on Ubuntu/Linux

patterns.explanation: Installs the snap from the Snap Store

patterns.text: install a classic snap without confinement
patterns.explanation: Installs VS Code in classic mode with full system access
Shows all installed snap packages and their versions
Updates all installed snaps to their latest versions
Installs GIMP from the Flathub repository
Launches the installed Flatpak application
Shows all installed Flatpak applications and runtimes
Checks for and installs updates for all Flatpak apps
Downloads and installs the package from PyPI

patterns.text: install packages from a requirements file
patterns.explanation: Installs all packages listed in the requirements file

patterns.text: upgrade a package to the latest version
patterns.explanation: Updates the specified package to the newest version
Shows all installed packages and their versions

patterns.text: freeze installed packages into a requirements file
patterns.explanation: Exports exact package versions for reproducibility

patterns.text: install a package for Python 3 specifically
patterns.explanation: Installs the package into the Python 3 environment

patterns.text: install a package for the current user only
patterns.explanation: Installs to the user directory without needing sudo

patterns.text: show details about an installed package
patterns.explanation: Displays version, location, dependencies, and metadata
Removes the specified package
Reads package.json and installs all listed dependencies

patterns.text: add a new package to the project
patterns.explanation: Installs express and adds it to package.json

patterns.text: install a package as a dev dependency
patterns.explanation: Adds jest to devDependencies in package.json
Installs the package system-wide for CLI usage

patterns.text: run a script defined in package.json
patterns.explanation: Executes the build script from the scripts section

commands.name: npx
commands.description: Execute npm package binaries without installing globally

patterns.text: run a package without installing it
patterns.explanation: Downloads and runs create-react-app temporarily

patterns.text: run a specific version of a package
patterns.explanation: Uses a specific Node.js version for a one-off command
Runs the project-local jest binary

patterns.text: execute a GitHub gist as a script
patterns.explanation: Downloads and runs the gist as a Node.js script

commands.name: yarn
commands.description: Fast, reliable, and secure dependency management for JavaScript

patterns.text: install all project dependencies with Yarn
patterns.explanation: Installs dependencies from yarn.lock for deterministic builds

patterns.text: add a package to the project
patterns.explanation: Installs lodash and adds it to package.json
Adds eslint as a development dependency
Uninstalls the package and updates package.json

patterns.text: upgrade all packages to their latest versions
patterns.explanation: Updates all dependencies to the newest available versions
Downloads and installs the Rails gem
Shows all locally installed gems and their versions

patterns.text: update a gem to the latest version
patterns.explanation: Upgrades bundler to the newest available version
Removes the specified gem from the system

commands.name: cargo
commands.description: Package manager and build system for Rust

patterns.explanation: Scaffolds a new Rust binary project with Cargo.toml
Compiles the project with optimizations enabled
Compiles and immediately executes the binary

patterns.text: install a Rust binary crate globally
patterns.explanation: Compiles and installs the binary to ~/.cargo/bin
Compiles and runs all tests in the project

commands.name: go install
commands.description: Compile and install Go packages and binaries

patterns.explanation: Downloads, compiles, and installs the binary to GOPATH/bin

patterns.text: install a specific version of a Go tool
patterns.explanation: Installs a pinned version of the linter

patterns.text: install all dependencies for a Go module
patterns.explanation: Downloads all module dependencies to the local cache

patterns.text: add a dependency to a Go project
patterns.explanation: Adds the package as a dependency and downloads it
Removes unused dependencies and adds missing ones
